title: 🔒 [security fix] Hardened configuration file permissions and TOCTOU protection
body:
🎯 **What:** Fixed an insecure permissions fallback vulnerability that was susceptible to Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) attacks when creating or updating configuration files.

⚠️ **Risk:** An attacker with local access could potentially exploit a race condition using symbolic links to cause the application to modify permissions on sensitive system files instead of the intended configuration file.

🛡️ **Solution:** 
- Added `os.O_NOFOLLOW` to `os.open` flags to prevent following symbolic links during the initial file opening.
- Implemented a secure, multi-stage permission enforcement strategy that prioritizes operating on the file descriptor (`fchmod` or `chmod` on FD) before falling back to path-based `chmod` only when necessary for platform compatibility.

Verified on Linux with a custom test script confirming `fchmod` and `O_NOFOLLOW` behavior.

══════════ ELIR ══════════
PURPOSE: Hardens configuration file creation and permission setting against local race conditions (TOCTOU).
SECURITY: Prevents symlink attacks via `O_NOFOLLOW` and ensures permissions are set on the actual file descriptor, not a potentially swapped path.
FAILS IF: The platform supports neither `fchmod` nor `chmod` on FD, and a race condition is perfectly timed during the path-based fallback.
VERIFY: Check `src/utils/setup_wizard.py` and `src/app_runner.py` for the new `os.open` flags and the `try...except` block sequence for permissions.
MAINTAIN: `os.O_NOFOLLOW` is key for preventing symlink following at open-time; keep it in all file-creation paths handling sensitive data.
head: security-fix-toctou-permissions
